mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, with support for various hardware configurations. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and the complexity of managing model deployments.

steps.title: Install the vLLM CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM command-line interface, which is necessary for managing vLLM deployments.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.18.1

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This allows kubectl to communicate with your cluster.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in a 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Deploy vLLM on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys vLLM on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.1

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --version 0.18.1 --namespace vllm --create-namespace

```

> Add the vLLM Helm repository.
> Install vLLM in the vllm namespace.

steps.title: Post-installation configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This command removes the vLLM deployment and may result in data loss. Ensure to backup any important data before proceeding.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This removes the vLLM release.
> This deletes the entire cluster and associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to backup your configurations and data. This process involves draining nodes, upgrading the Helm chart, and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --version 0.18.1 --namespace vllm

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrade to the new version of vLLM.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.
> Verify that all pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or add more nodes to the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM pod is failing to start due to misconfiguration or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service is not reachable, possibly due to incorrect service configuration.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure the pods are running.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new decoding algorithms and improved memory management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME                     STATUS   ROLES    AGE   VERSION' with all nodes in 'Ready' state when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
